dataset: Bay Area water quality monitoring stations (synthetic)

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates CSV at this exact path (lines 26-36)
export_result.sh sets OUTPUT_PATH to this location (line 9)
setup_task.sh creates exactly 8 water station records in CSV (lines 27-36)
Voronoi diagrams/Thiessen polygons create polygon geometries. Verified via QGIS documentation: Vector > Geometry Tools > Voronoi Polygons generates Polygon or MultiPolygon geometries.
CSV header in setup_task.sh (line 27) shows all 7 columns; required attributes are subset of available columns

pi_items.metadata_value: USGS-based monitoring stations in the San Francisco Bay Area
pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data created by setup script, using realistic water body names and coordinates, but not directly from USGS database
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates data inline with 'cat' heredoc (lines 26-36), not downloaded from external source. Station IDs reference USGS naming convention but don't all match actual USGS stations.

pi_items.verified_value: All values are within realistic ranges for San Francisco Bay Area estuary water quality
pi_items.source: Web search verified: pH regulatory standard for SF Bay is 6.5-8.5; typical dissolved oxygen in healthy estuaries 5-8 mg/L; temperatures 15-18°C consistent with Bay Area conditions documented by USGS Water Quality of San Francisco Bay Research Program

pi_items.metadata_value: Eight stations distributed across San Francisco Bay Area
pi_items.verified_value: Coordinates place stations in realistic Bay Area locations: Alviso (~37.43, -121.97), Guadalupe River (~37.35, -121.93), San Leandro Bay (~37.80, -122.39), Oakland Harbor (~37.73, -122.17), etc.
pi_items.source: USGS NWIS mapping and coordinate searches confirmed these are plausible locations for Bay Area water monitoring

privileged_info_summary: This task uses an 8-feature synthetic water quality dataset created during setup (not downloaded from external source). The dataset mimics real USGS Bay Area monitoring stations with plausible coordinates, water body names, and chemistry values (pH 7.3-7.9, dissolved oxygen 5.5-8.3 mg/L, temperature 15-18°C) that fall within documented ranges for San Francisco Bay estuary waters. The expected outputs are correct: 8 Voronoi polygon features exported as GeoJSON with preserved attributes (station_name, ph, dissolved_oxygen_mg_l) from the input CSV.
